code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment based solely on the review text
    - Base rating on holistic satisfaction, not isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multi-purchase failures overriding positives
    - Premature failure (≤6mo) with functional consequences
    - Negative updates about same product erasing satisfaction
    - "Never again" declarations with defect evidence

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires constant adjustments
    - Persistent frustration during regular use
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - Purchase regret with detailed justification

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with usage limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with clear compromises

    4★ (Positive):
    - Factual feature listings without enthusiastic language
    - Neutral functionality confirmation ("works fine")
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("4 because...")
    - Brand comparisons with minor shortcomings
    - Two functional benefits without endorsement

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Enthusiastic adjectives ("amazing", "perfect") + benefits
    - 3+ functional benefits with validation
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite non-functional flaws
    - Replacement purchases indicating satisfaction
    - Practical benefits overriding all criticisms
    - Targeted recommendations solving specific needs

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Functional flaw + core praise →4 / Non-functional flaw →5
    - "Works as intended" + enthusiasm →5 / neutral →4
    - Negative updates →1★ only if about same product
    - Multi-aspect criticism + final endorsement →5
    - Primary use limitation →4 / alternative suitability →5
    - Explicit overall rating declarations override components
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "This mattress: 4 stars comfort, 5 stars features overall"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Wax and wicks. They work just fine."
<Reasoning>Neutral functionality confirmation →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "I like this towel. Turkish cotton, good absorbency, competitive price."
<Reasoning>Factual feature listing →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "This rug is SO plush! Dog-approved and eco-friendly."
<Reasoning>Enthusiastic adjectives + benefits →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Village Candle equals Yankee but scent fades faster"
<Reasoning>Brand comparison with shortcoming →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Good for light use but material feels cheap"
<Reasoning>Clear compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Weekly adjustments needed for basic function"
<Reasoning>Persistent maintenance →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "UPDATE: Failed after 3mo. Never again this brand"
<Reasoning>Definitive failure update →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Color isn't perfect but works amazingly well daily"
<Reasoning>Non-functional flaw + core praise →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (32 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 13
  gold 5 -> 4: 10
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 1 -> 5: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  5. Review: "This really is a beautiful mug. It's so beautiful I am thinking of just using it as decor rather than actually using it. It's not really big enough to use for my morning cup of coffee (I need a BIG mug for that), although it's the perfect size for my..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I like my Star Wars characters. I have a Darth Vader mug already and now I have the Chewbacca. The cup is nice and I like that it has the sash detail on the front and back so you know immediately it is Chewie. The mug can hold 20 ounces. As with most..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "While this looks cool, it functionally is no good.  To grate cheese, you have to hold onto the top of the taco.  Because it is plastic, the two sides squeeze together when gripped. This, coupled with the slipperiness of the plastic, makes this thing ..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Made in the USA.  It's the only candles I'll buy since I've just read too much about what can be in foreign made candles.  Things like lead. (ugh)

Normally I buy Yankee candles but I found this Village Candle to be just as nice if not nicer.  The sc..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I can forgive the fact that there was blood on one of the math mats when I opened the package.  Things happen and I'm sorry that someone was injured.  What I can't get past is the fact that there are so many snags.  It's like these two mats were seco..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "This looks like a great idea and easy to use, but actually it's very awkward. The idea is to hang your jewelry in the closet. But the hanger part doesn't swivel, so if you want to select an item, you have to remove it from the closet and lay it on th..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33

Summary: 32 errors, 16 borderline out of 80 examples."}
